verses.text: Fill ye waters for yourselves from the living fountain, of the Lord, for it is opened to you:
And come all ye thirsty and take the draught; and rest by the fountain of the Lord.
For fair it is and pure and gives rest to the soul, Much more pleasant are its waters than honey;
And the honeycomb of bees is not to be compared with it.

blurb: In a world filled with competing voices and endless streams of information, these ancient words remind us where true refreshment is found. The invitation to drink from the Lord's fountain speaks to our deepest thirst—not for more news or noise, but for what actually nourishes the soul. This Friday, amid all the distractions that vie for our attention, we're called to pause and drink deeply from the source that satisfies like nothing else can.
